description:
# .43.H — lower_pattern_bind becomes a 1-row matrix wrapper

## What changes

`lower_pattern_bind` (src/ir_lower.rs ~1907):

```rust
fn lower_pattern_bind(ctx, subject, pat, fail_block) -> Result {
    let matrix = Matrix {
        subjects: vec![subject],
        rows: vec![Row {
            patterns: vec![pat.clone()],
            guard: None,
            body_id: 0,  // unused at this single-row position
        }],
    };
    lower_pattern_matrix(ctx, matrix, fail_block, |_, _, _| Ok(()))
}
```

The matrix lowerer (introduced in .D) handles ALL pattern shapes:
- Constructors (Tuple, Atom, Int, etc.) → matrix specialization
- Bitstring / Map → PerRow → existing single-pattern lowering routines
  extracted into reusable helpers (lower_bitstring_pattern,
  lower_map_pattern) so PerRow can invoke them directly

## What stays

- Function signature `lower_pattern_bind(ctx, subject, pat, fail_block)`
- All call sites: multi-head match (lines 877, 1218, 1682), with's
  `<-` binding (line 2535) — unchanged
- All semantics: single-pattern bind succeeds or jumps to fail_block

## What's removed

- ~200 LOC: the body of lower_pattern_bind (Tuple, List, Map, As,
  Bitstring branches)
- The standalone `Prim::TypeTest` emission in Pattern::Tuple branch
  (line 1955) — subsumed by matrix's Switch lowering
- The `next: BlockId` threading through lower_pattern_bind recursion
  (the matrix knows fail_block)
- The duplication between lower_pattern_bind's Pattern::Tuple branch
  and the matrix's TupleArity Switch handling

## Acceptance

- Single-pattern positions (let-binding, multi-head, with `<-`):
  golden byte-identical
- Bitstring fixtures: byte-identical (PerRow → extracted bitstring
  helper produces same IR as today)
- Map fixtures: byte-identical
- Three-path parity
- Net LOC reduction in ir_lower.rs (verify; should be ~150 lines
  smaller after extracting bitstring/map helpers)

## Risk

Bitstring patterns are the most special-cased. The reader-threading
logic (BitReaderInit + per-field BitReadField + extracted-value bind)
must be byte-identical post-extraction. Strategy: extract first as a
mechanical no-op refactor, verify goldens, THEN switch
lower_pattern_bind body to the 1-row matrix. Two safe steps in one
ticket.
title: fz-ul4.43.H: lower_pattern_bind becomes 1-row matrix wrapper
